behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-loader handoff: the loader must block module import when manifest validation fails (MOCK-MISS: cross-module interaction, not just one pure function). Minimum infra is multiple in-process modules plus fixture plugins on local filesystem to detect accidental import side effects. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require subprocess/real network/OS-only primitives; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML/LLM decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose manifest omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel side-effect file when imported. Run loader over directory and assert: (1) validation error for that plugin, (2) sentinel file is absent, (3) plugin onLoad is never called.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears when dependency resolver output is consumed by lifecycle runner (MOCK-MISS: module boundary). A unit test of topological sort alone could miss execution-order bugs in hook invocation. Rejected higher levels: System disqualified by behavior text (no subprocess/network/external API); Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI/ML output dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Set up two fixture plugins A and B in a temp directory, with A depending on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory/event log. Run loader and assert observed call sequence is [B, A].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection must integrate with load gatekeeping so neither plugin is initialized; failure is resolver-to-executor interaction, not isolated algorithm only. Rejected higher levels: System disqualified (no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text); Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI/ML dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A. Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error/report includes A<->B, (2) neither entry module side effect occurs, (3) neither onLoad is invoked.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit sequencing across import system and lifecycle hook dispatcher; a single-function unit test cannot catch misordered real execution. Rejected higher levels: System disqualified because behavior text only requires local module import ordering; no subprocess/network. Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI/ML model call or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a fixture plugin whose entry module sets global/state flag on import, and whose onLoad asserts that flag is already set. Run loader and assert onLoad succeeds and recorded order is import-side-effect before onLoad.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Requires wiring of dependency graph, error isolation, and continuation logic across multiple plugin loads; this failure emerges in orchestrated module interaction. Rejected higher levels: System disqualified by behavior text (no subprocess/real network/OS primitive dependence). Agentic/Workflow disqualified because correctness does not depend on AI/ML output.
behaviors.test_description: Create three plugins: F (throws in onLoad), D (depends on F), and I (independent). Run loader and assert: (1) F error is reported, (2) D is skipped/not loaded due to dependency on F, (3) I onLoad still runs successfully.
